content.content:
1	# Story 3.4: Auto-Shard at 400-Line Ceiling
2	
3	Status: ready-for-dev
4	
5	## Story
6	
7	As a skill author,
8	I want SKILL.md bodies exceeding 400 lines to be automatically sharded into references,
9	So that skills stay within the 500-line skill-check ceiling with comfortable headroom.
10	
11	## Acceptance Criteria
12	
13	1. **Given** a compiled SKILL.md with a body exceeding 400 lines
14	   **When** the auto-shard step runs post-compile
15	   **Then** navigational catalogs and deep-dive sections are moved to `references/`
16	   **And** the SKILL.md body is reduced to under 400 lines
17	   **And** hallucination guards (T1 markers, confidence annotations) remain inline in SKILL.md
18	
19	2. **Given** a compiled SKILL.md with a body of 350 lines
20	   **When** the auto-shard step runs post-compile
21	   **Then** no sharding is triggered and SKILL.md is unchanged
22	
23	3. **Given** a sharded SKILL.md
24	   **When** the resulting skill is validated by skill-check
25	   **Then** the skill passes the 500-line ceiling check
26	   **And** cross-references between SKILL.md and sharded reference files are intact
27	
28	## Tasks / Subtasks
29	
30	- [ ] Task 1: Create step-auto-shard.md step file (AC: #1, #2, #3)
31	  - [ ] 1.1 Create `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md` with frontmatter `nextStepFile: 'validate.md'`
32	  - [ ] 1.2 Implement §1 (Count Body Lines): count lines between frontmatter closing `---` and EOF (exclude trailing blank lines). Store as `body_line_count`. If ≤ 400 → skip to chain (proceed to validate.md)
33	  - [ ] 1.3 Implement §2 (Selective Shard — Tier 2 Extraction): identify Tier 2 sections by `## Full` heading prefix (`## Full API Reference`, `## Full Type Definitions`, `## Full Integration Patterns`). Extract to `references/` starting with largest. Replace extracted section with a cross-reference line: `> See [Full API Reference](references/full-api-reference.md)` (kebab-case filename)
34	  - [ ] 1.4 Implement §3 (Tier 1 Preservation Check): verify ALL Tier 1 sections remain inline after extraction — Overview, Quick Start, Common Workflows, Key API Summary, Migration & Deprecation Warnings (if present), Key Types, Architecture at a Glance, CLI (if present), Scripts & Assets (if present), Manual Sections. HALT if any Tier 1 section was moved
35	  - [ ] 1.5 Implement §4 (Post-Shard Validation): re-count body lines; if still > 400 after extracting all Tier 2 sections, reduce oversized Tier 1 content (Key API Summary, Architecture at a Glance) — do NOT extract Tier 1 to references
36	  - [ ] 1.6 Implement §5 (Cross-Reference Integrity): verify each extracted reference file exists in `references/` directory and each cross-reference link in SKILL.md resolves correctly
37	  - [ ] 1.7 Log auto-shard results to context: `auto_shard_triggered: true/false`, `sections_extracted: [names]`, `body_lines_before`, `body_lines_after`
38	
39	- [ ] Task 2: Update step chain — step-doc-sources.md (AC: #1, #2)
40	  - [ ] 2.1 Change `nextStepFile` in `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-sources.md` frontmatter from `'validate.md'` to `'step-auto-shard.md'`
41	
42	- [ ] Task 3: Update CS SKILL.md Stages table (AC: #1, #2)
43	  - [ ] 3.1 Add row after step 5a: `| 5b | Auto-Shard | references/step-auto-shard.md | Yes |`
44	
45	- [ ] Task 4: Create structural integration tests (AC: #1, #2, #3)
46	  - [ ] 4.1 Create `test/test-skf-auto-shard.py` following the pattern from `test/test-skf-evidence-report-fallback.py`
47	  - [ ] 4.2 Register the new test file in `package.json` test:python command
48	  - [ ] 4.3 Test cases: step-auto-shard.md exists, nextStepFile is validate.md, 400-line threshold constant present, Tier 1 section names listed for preservation, Tier 2 `## Full` heading pattern present, cross-reference link format present, step-doc-sources.md chains to step-auto-shard.md, SKILL.md Stages table includes Auto-Shard row, step-auto-shard.md has §-prefixed sections, context logging variables present (auto_shard_triggered, sections_extracted, body_lines_before, body_lines_after)
49	
50	## Dev Notes
51	
52	### Where Auto-Shard Fits in the Step Chain
53	
54	The auto-shard step slots between doc-sources (5a) and validate (6). The updated chain:
55	
56	```
57	compile.md → step-doc-sources.md → step-auto-shard.md → validate.md → generate-artifacts.md → report.md → health-check.md
58	```
59	
60	The auto-shard at 400 lines is a **proactive** size reduction. validate.md §4 (which triggers on skill-check's 500-line `body.max_lines` failure) remains as a **safety net**. In most cases, auto-shard handles the extraction and validate §4 becomes a no-op.
61	
62	### Relationship to validate.md §4 (split-body)
63	
64	validate.md §4 already handles oversized bodies at the 500-line ceiling with selective Tier 2 extraction. The auto-shard step at 400 lines:
65	- Uses the **same Tier 2 selective extraction approach** (extract `## Full` sections starting with largest)
66	- Follows the **same Tier 1 preservation invariant** (Tier 1 sections must never move to references/)
67	- Runs **earlier in the chain** to provide headroom below the 500-line hard limit
68	- Does NOT use `npx skill-check split-body --write` — it is a step-file-level extraction, not a tool invocation
69	- Does NOT invoke the Description Guard Protocol — it does not modify frontmatter, only body content
70	
71	If auto-shard already reduced the body to under 400 lines, validate.md §4 will see no `body.max_lines` failure from skill-check and skip its split logic entirely.
72	
73	### Tier 1 vs Tier 2 Section Names
74	
75	**Tier 1 — Always Inline (never extract):**
76	- Overview, Quick Start, Common Workflows, Key API Summary, Migration & Deprecation Warnings (conditional), Key Types, Architecture at a Glance, CLI (conditional), Scripts & Assets (conditional), Manual Sections
77	
78	**Tier 2 — Reference-Eligible (extract targets):**
79	- `## Full API Reference` → `references/full-api-reference.md`
80	- `## Full Type Definitions` → `references/full-type-definitions.md`
81	- `## Full Integration Patterns` → `references/full-integration-patterns.md`
82	
83	Identification: Tier 2 sections have the `## Full` heading prefix. This is the same heuristic used by validate.md §4.
84	
85	### Cross-Reference Format
86	
87	When a Tier 2 section is extracted, replace it in SKILL.md with a blockquote cross-reference:
88	
89	```markdown
90	> See [Full API Reference](references/full-api-reference.md)
91	```
92	
93	This matches the existing pattern used by validate.md §4's selective extraction. The reference file contains the complete section content with its `##` heading preserved.
94	
95	### Body Line Counting
96	
97	"Body" = all lines after the frontmatter closing `---` to EOF (excluding trailing blank lines). This matches the metric that `skill-check` uses for `body.max_lines`. The 400-line threshold provides 100 lines of headroom below the 500-line ceiling.
98	
99	### What Must NOT Change
100	
101	- `validate.md` — the §4 split-body logic stays as-is (safety net at 500 lines)
102	- `compile.md` — compile step is unchanged; auto-shard runs after compile, not during
103	- `generate-artifacts.md` — writes whatever SKILL.md + references/ exist post-validate; no changes needed (it already handles `ref_count >= 0`)
104	- The Description Guard Protocol — auto-shard does not touch frontmatter, only body sections
105	- `compile-assembly-rules.md` — assembly rules unchanged; auto-shard is post-assembly
106	
107	### Step File Conventions (from Stories 3.1–3.3)
108	
109	1. Frontmatter with `nextStepFile` for chaining
110	2. `# Step 5b: Auto-Shard` heading matching the Stages table
111	3. `## STEP GOAL:` heading
112	4. `## Rules` section
113	5. `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` with §-prefixed subsections
114	6. Auto-proceed step — no user interaction gates
115	7. Graceful failure — if body is under threshold, skip cleanly
116	8. Self-audit: NO internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in step file content
117	
118	### Auto-Shard Algorithm
119	
120	```
121	§1 Count body lines
122	  IF body_lines <= 400 → log "auto-shard: skipped (body {N} lines)", chain to validate.md
123	  ELSE → proceed to §2
124	
125	§2 Selective Tier 2 extraction
126	  FOR EACH Tier 2 section (sorted by line count DESC):
127	    - Extract section content to references/{kebab-case-name}.md
128	    - Replace section in SKILL.md with cross-reference blockquote
129	    - Re-count body lines
130	    - IF body_lines <= 400 → stop extracting, proceed to §3
131	
132	§3 Tier 1 preservation check
133	  FOR EACH known Tier 1 heading:
134	    - Verify heading exists in SKILL.md body
135	    - IF missing → HALT: "Auto-shard removed Tier 1 section {name}. Aborting."
136	
137	§4 Post-shard validation
138	  Re-count body lines
139	  IF still > 400 after all Tier 2 extracted:
140	    - Trim oversized Tier 1 sections (Key API Summary, Architecture at a Glance)
141	    - Do NOT move Tier 1 to references
142	
143	§5 Cross-reference integrity
144	  FOR EACH extracted reference file:
145	    - Verify file exists at expected path
146	    - Verify cross-reference link in SKILL.md points to it
147	  Log: "auto-shard: {N} sections extracted, body reduced from {before} to {after} lines"
148	```
149	
150	### Story 3.3 Learnings (Apply These)
151	
152	1. **Self-audit for internal references**: Do NOT include internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in the step file content or commit messages
153	2. **Cross-reference grep when modifying step files**: After modifying step-doc-sources.md and creating step-auto-shard.md, grep for any other files that reference the step chain order or `step-doc-sources.md` → `validate.md` chaining
154	3. **Section numbering convention**: Use §-prefixed sections (§1, §2, etc.) within the step file
155	4. **Register test file in package.json**: The `test:python` command enumerates every test path — adding `test/test-skf-auto-shard.py` without updating package.json leaves it dormant in CI
156

# Story 3.4: Auto-Shard at 400-Line Ceiling

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a skill author,
I want SKILL.md bodies exceeding 400 lines to be automatically sharded into references,
So that skills stay within the 500-line skill-check ceiling with comfortable headroom.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a compiled SKILL.md with a body exceeding 400 lines
   **When** the auto-shard step runs post-compile
   **Then** navigational catalogs and deep-dive sections are moved to `references/`
   **And** the SKILL.md body is reduced to under 400 lines
   **And** hallucination guards (T1 markers, confidence annotations) remain inline in SKILL.md

2. **Given** a compiled SKILL.md with a body of 350 lines
   **When** the auto-shard step runs post-compile
   **Then** no sharding is triggered and SKILL.md is unchanged

3. **Given** a sharded SKILL.md
   **When** the resulting skill is validated by skill-check
   **Then** the skill passes the 500-line ceiling check
   **And** cross-references between SKILL.md and sharded reference files are intact

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Create step-auto-shard.md step file (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [ ] 1.1 Create `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md` with frontmatter `nextStepFile: 'validate.md'`
  - [ ] 1.2 Implement §1 (Count Body Lines): count lines between frontmatter closing `---` and EOF (exclude trailing blank lines). Store as `body_line_count`. If ≤ 400 → skip to chain (proceed to validate.md)
  - [ ] 1.3 Implement §2 (Selective Shard — Tier 2 Extraction): identify Tier 2 sections by `## Full` heading prefix (`## Full API Reference`, `## Full Type Definitions`, `## Full Integration Patterns`). Extract to `references/` starting with largest. Replace extracted section with a cross-reference line: `> See [Full API Reference](references/full-api-reference.md)` (kebab-case filename)
  - [ ] 1.4 Implement §3 (Tier 1 Preservation Check): verify ALL Tier 1 sections remain inline after extraction — Overview, Quick Start, Common Workflows, Key API Summary, Migration & Deprecation Warnings (if present), Key Types, Architecture at a Glance, CLI (if present), Scripts & Assets (if present), Manual Sections. HALT if any Tier 1 section was moved
  - [ ] 1.5 Implement §4 (Post-Shard Validation): re-count body lines; if still > 400 after extracting all Tier 2 sections, reduce oversized Tier 1 content (Key API Summary, Architecture at a Glance) — do NOT extract Tier 1 to references
  - [ ] 1.6 Implement §5 (Cross-Reference Integrity): verify each extracted reference file exists in `references/` directory and each cross-reference link in SKILL.md resolves correctly
  - [ ] 1.7 Log auto-shard results to context: `auto_shard_triggered: true/false`, `sections_extracted: [names]`, `body_lines_before`, `body_lines_after`

- [ ] Task 2: Update step chain — step-doc-sources.md (AC: #1, #2)
  - [ ] 2.1 Change `nextStepFile` in `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-sources.md` frontmatter from `'validate.md'` to `'step-auto-shard.md'`

- [ ] Task 3: Update CS SKILL.md Stages table (AC: #1, #2)
  - [ ] 3.1 Add row after step 5a: `| 5b | Auto-Shard | references/step-auto-shard.md | Yes |`

- [ ] Task 4: Create structural integration tests (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [ ] 4.1 Create `test/test-skf-auto-shard.py` following the pattern from `test/test-skf-evidence-report-fallback.py`
  - [ ] 4.2 Register the new test file in `package.json` test:python command
  - [ ] 4.3 Test cases: step-auto-shard.md exists, nextStepFile is validate.md, 400-line threshold constant present, Tier 1 section names listed for preservation, Tier 2 `## Full` heading pattern present, cross-reference link format present, step-doc-sources.md chains to step-auto-shard.md, SKILL.md Stages table includes Auto-Shard row, step-auto-shard.md has §-prefixed sections, context logging variables present (auto_shard_triggered, sections_extracted, body_lines_before, body_lines_after)

## Dev Notes

### Where Auto-Shard Fits in the Step Chain

The auto-shard step slots between doc-sources (5a) and validate (6). The updated chain:

```
compile.md → step-doc-sources.md → step-auto-shard.md → validate.md → generate-artifacts.md → report.md → health-check.md
```

The auto-shard at 400 lines is a **proactive** size reduction. validate.md §4 (which triggers on skill-check's 500-line `body.max_lines` failure) remains as a **safety net**. In most cases, auto-shard handles the extraction and validate §4 becomes a no-op.

### Relationship to validate.md §4 (split-body)

validate.md §4 already handles oversized bodies at the 500-line ceiling with selective Tier 2 extraction. The auto-shard step at 400 lines:
- Uses the **same Tier 2 selective extraction approach** (extract `## Full` sections starting with largest)
- Follows the **same Tier 1 preservation invariant** (Tier 1 sections must never move to references/)
- Runs **earlier in the chain** to provide headroom below the 500-line hard limit
- Does NOT use `npx skill-check split-body --write` — it is a step-file-level extraction, not a tool invocation
- Does NOT invoke the Description Guard Protocol — it does not modify frontmatter, only body content

If auto-shard already reduced the body to under 400 lines, validate.md §4 will see no `body.max_lines` failure from skill-check and skip its split logic entirely.

### Tier 1 vs Tier 2 Section Names

**Tier 1 — Always Inline (never extract):**
- Overview, Quick Start, Common Workflows, Key API Summary, Migration & Deprecation Warnings (conditional), Key Types, Architecture at a Glance, CLI (conditional), Scripts & Assets (conditional), Manual Sections

**Tier 2 — Reference-Eligible (extract targets):**
- `## Full API Reference` → `references/full-api-reference.md`
- `## Full Type Definitions` → `references/full-type-definitions.md`
- `## Full Integration Patterns` → `references/full-integration-patterns.md`

Identification: Tier 2 sections have the `## Full` heading prefix. This is the same heuristic used by validate.md §4.

### Cross-Reference Format

When a Tier 2 section is extracted, replace it in SKILL.md with a blockquote cross-reference:

```markdown
> See [Full API Reference](references/full-api-reference.md)
```

This matches the existing pattern used by validate.md §4's selective extraction. The reference file contains the complete section content with its `##` heading preserved.

### Body Line Counting

"Body" = all lines after the frontmatter closing `---` to EOF (excluding trailing blank lines). This matches the metric that `skill-check` uses for `body.max_lines`. The 400-line threshold provides 100 lines of headroom below the 500-line ceiling.

### What Must NOT Change

- `validate.md` — the §4 split-body logic stays as-is (safety net at 500 lines)
- `compile.md` — compile step is unchanged; auto-shard runs after compile, not during
- `generate-artifacts.md` — writes whatever SKILL.md + references/ exist post-validate; no changes needed (it already handles `ref_count >= 0`)
- The Description Guard Protocol — auto-shard does not touch frontmatter, only body sections
- `compile-assembly-rules.md` — assembly rules unchanged; auto-shard is post-assembly

### Step File Conventions (from Stories 3.1–3.3)

1. Frontmatter with `nextStepFile` for chaining
2. `# Step 5b: Auto-Shard` heading matching the Stages table
3. `## STEP GOAL:` heading
4. `## Rules` section
5. `## MANDATORY SEQUENCE` with §-prefixed subsections
6. Auto-proceed step — no user interaction gates
7. Graceful failure — if body is under threshold, skip cleanly
8. Self-audit: NO internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in step file content

### Auto-Shard Algorithm

```
§1 Count body lines
  IF body_lines <= 400 → log "auto-shard: skipped (body {N} lines)", chain to validate.md
  ELSE → proceed to §2

§2 Selective Tier 2 extraction
  FOR EACH Tier 2 section (sorted by line count DESC):
    - Extract section content to references/{kebab-case-name}.md
    - Replace section in SKILL.md with cross-reference blockquote
    - Re-count body lines
    - IF body_lines <= 400 → stop extracting, proceed to §3

§3 Tier 1 preservation check
  FOR EACH known Tier 1 heading:
    - Verify heading exists in SKILL.md body
    - IF missing → HALT: "Auto-shard removed Tier 1 section {name}. Aborting."

§4 Post-shard validation
  Re-count body lines
  IF still > 400 after all Tier 2 extracted:
    - Trim oversized Tier 1 sections (Key API Summary, Architecture at a Glance)
    - Do NOT move Tier 1 to references

§5 Cross-reference integrity
  FOR EACH extracted reference file:
    - Verify file exists at expected path
    - Verify cross-reference link in SKILL.md points to it
  Log: "auto-shard: {N} sections extracted, body reduced from {before} to {after} lines"
```

### Story 3.3 Learnings (Apply These)

1. **Self-audit for internal references**: Do NOT include internal story IDs, epic numbers, or bmad keywords in the step file content or commit messages
2. **Cross-reference grep when modifying step files**: After modifying step-doc-sources.md and creating step-auto-shard.md, grep for any other files that reference the step chain order or `step-doc-sources.md` → `validate.md` chaining
3. **Section numbering convention**: Use §-prefixed sections (§1, §2, etc.) within the step file
4. **Register test file in package.json**: The `test:python` command enumerates every test path — adding `test/test-skf-auto-shard.py` without updating package.json leaves it dormant in CI

### Story 3.1 Learnings (Apply These)

1. **Report.md stepsCompleted chain**: Story 3.1 discovered that report.md in TS hardcodes a canonical step chain. Check whether CS's report.md has a similar pattern — if it does, ensure 'auto-shard' is added
2. **Stages table alignment**: Step numbering in the Stages table must match the `# Step N:` heading in the step file

### Cross-Reference Check

After creating step-auto-shard.md and modifying step-doc-sources.md:
- `src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md` — Stages table must list step 5b
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-sources.md` — nextStepFile must be step-auto-shard.md
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/validate.md` — §4 remains unchanged (safety net)
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/report.md` — check for hardcoded step chain/stepsCompleted
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/generate-artifacts.md` — `ref_count` handling already supports 0 or N references; no changes needed
- `src/skf-create-skill/assets/compile-assembly-rules.md` — mentions split-body in assembly rules; no changes needed (auto-shard is a separate pre-validate step, not a compile-time concern)

### Project Structure Notes

- New file: `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md` — the auto-shard step
- Modified: `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-sources.md` — nextStepFile chain update
- Modified: `src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md` — Stages table row addition
- New file: `test/test-skf-auto-shard.py` — structural integration tests
- Modified: `package.json` — test:python registration

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md — Story 3.4 "Auto-shard at 400-line ceiling"]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Quality Gate Architecture" section: "Auto-shard trigger: CS/SS post-compile step"]
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md — "Modified Workflows" section: `step-auto-shard.md` in CS references]
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/SKILL.md — Stages table, step chain, Invocation Contract]
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/compile.md — nextStepFile: step-doc-sources.md]
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-sources.md — nextStepFile: validate.md (to be changed)]
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/references/validate.md — §4 Split Oversized Body, selective extraction, Tier 1 preservation]
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/assets/skill-sections.md — Tier 1 and Tier 2 section definitions]
- [Source: src/skf-create-skill/assets/compile-assembly-rules.md — two-tier assembly rules, split-body references]
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-3-evidence-report-on-threshold-fallback.md — Story 3.3 learnings]

## deepwiki
DeepWiki MCP provides AI-powered documentation for GitHub repositories.

Available tools:
- read_wiki_structure: Get a list of documentation topics for a repository
- read_wiki_contents: View full documentation about a repository
- ask_question: Ask any question about a repository and get an AI-powered answer
- list_available_repos: List your available repositories (private mode only)
- generate_wiki: Generate a codebase wiki for a repository — only use when explicitly requested by the user (private mode only)
- devin_knowledge_manage: Manage Devin knowledge notes and suggestions — list, search, get, create, update, delete notes, view folder structure, list/view/dismiss knowledge suggestions (private mode only)
- devin_playbook_manage: Manage Devin playbooks — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)
- devin_schedule_manage: Manage scheduled Devin sessions — list, get, create, update, delete (private mode only)

- devin_session_search: Search and filter Devin sessions (private mode only)

1	---
2	name: skf-create-skill
3	description: Compile a skill from a brief. Supports --batch for multiple briefs. Use when the user requests to "create a skill" or "compile a skill."
4	---
5	
6	# Create Skill
7	
8	## Overview
9	
10	Compiles a verified agent skill from a skill-brief.yaml and source code, producing an agentskills.io-compliant SKILL.md with provenance map, evidence report, and progressive disclosure references. The workflow is mostly autonomous with three interaction points — after ecosystem check (if match found), after source extraction (to confirm findings), and after content quality review (when tessl produces suggestions). Steps adapt behavior based on forge tier (Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep). Zero hallucination tolerance: every instruction in the output must trace to source code with a confidence tier citation.
11	
12	## Conventions
13	
14	- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
15	- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
16	- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
17	- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
18	- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.
19	
20	## Role
21	
22	You are operating in Ferris Architect mode — a skill compilation engine performing structural extraction and assembly. Apply zero hallucination tolerance: uncitable content is excluded, not guessed.
23	
24	## Workflow Rules
25	
26	These rules apply to every step in this workflow:
27	
28	- Never include content in SKILL.md that cannot be cited to source code
29	- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
30	- Always communicate in `{communication_language}`
31	- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision
32	
33	## Stages
34	
35	| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
36	|---|------|------|--------------|
37	| 1 | Load Brief | references/load-brief.md | Yes |
38	| 2 | Ecosystem Check | references/ecosystem-check.md | Conditional |
39	| 2b | CCC Discover | references/sub/ccc-discover.md | Yes |
40	| 3 | Extract | references/extract.md | No (confirm) |
41	| 3b | Fetch Temporal | references/sub/fetch-temporal.md | Yes |
42	| 3c | Fetch Docs | references/sub/fetch-docs.md | Yes |
43	| 3d | Component Extraction | references/component-extraction.md | Conditional |
44	| 4 | Enrich | references/enrich.md | Yes |
45	| 5 | Compile | references/compile.md | Yes |
46	| 5a | Doc Sources | references/step-doc-sources.md | Yes |
47	| 6 | Validate | references/validate.md | Conditional |
48	| 7 | Generate Artifacts | references/generate-artifacts.md | Yes |
49	| 8 | Report | references/report.md | Yes |
50	| 9 | Workflow Health Check | references/health-check.md | Yes |
51	
52	*Sub-steps under `references/sub/` are conditional branches (CCC discovery, temporal/doc enrichment) kept out of the top-level step count so main-line steps 1–9 drive the workflow. Step 3d (Component Extraction) stays top-level as an alternative main step that replaces the standard extraction path when `scope.type: "component-library"`.*
53	
54	## Invocation Contract
55	
56	| Aspect | Detail |
57	|--------|--------|
58	| **Inputs** | brief_path (path to skill-brief.yaml) [required], --batch [optional] |
59	| **Gates** | step 2: Choice Gate [P] (if match) | step 3: Review Gate [C] |
60	| **Outputs** | SKILL.md, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, provenance-map.json, evidence-report.md, references/ |
61	| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with default action when `{headless_mode}` is true |
62	
63	## On Activation
64	
65	1. Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and resolve:
66	   - `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`, `sidecar_path`, `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`
67	
68	2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in preferences.yaml. Default: false.
69	
70	3. Load, read the full file, and then execute `references/load-brief.md` to begin the workflow.

---
name: skf-create-skill
description: Compile a skill from a brief. Supports --batch for multiple briefs. Use when the user requests to "create a skill" or "compile a skill."
---

# Create Skill

## Overview

Compiles a verified agent skill from a skill-brief.yaml and source code, producing an agentskills.io-compliant SKILL.md with provenance map, evidence report, and progressive disclosure references. The workflow is mostly autonomous with three interaction points — after ecosystem check (if match found), after source extraction (to confirm findings), and after content quality review (when tessl produces suggestions). Steps adapt behavior based on forge tier (Quick/Forge/Forge+/Deep). Zero hallucination tolerance: every instruction in the output must trace to source code with a confidence tier citation.

## Conventions

- Bare paths (e.g. `references/<name>.md`) resolve from the skill root.
- `references/` holds prompt content carved out of SKILL.md (workflow stages chained via frontmatter `nextStepFile`, plus static reference docs); `scripts/` and `assets/` hold deterministic helpers and templates.
- `{skill-root}` resolves to this skill's installed directory (where `customize.toml` lives, if present).
- `{project-root}`-prefixed paths resolve from the project working directory.
- `{skill-name}` resolves to the skill directory's basename.

## Role

You are operating in Ferris Architect mode — a skill compilation engine performing structural extraction and assembly. Apply zero hallucination tolerance: uncitable content is excluded, not guessed.

## Workflow Rules

These rules apply to every step in this workflow:

- Never include content in SKILL.md that cannot be cited to source code
- Only load one step file at a time — never preload future steps
- Always communicate in `{communication_language}`
- If `{headless_mode}` is true, auto-proceed through confirmation gates with their default action and log each auto-decision

## Stages

| # | Step | File | Auto-proceed |
|---|------|------|--------------|
| 1 | Load Brief | references/load-brief.md | Yes |
| 2 | Ecosystem Check | references/ecosystem-check.md | Conditional |
| 2b | CCC Discover | references/sub/ccc-discover.md | Yes |
| 3 | Extract | references/extract.md | No (confirm) |
| 3b | Fetch Temporal | references/sub/fetch-temporal.md | Yes |
| 3c | Fetch Docs | references/sub/fetch-docs.md | Yes |
| 3d | Component Extraction | references/component-extraction.md | Conditional |
| 4 | Enrich | references/enrich.md | Yes |
| 5 | Compile | references/compile.md | Yes |
| 5a | Doc Sources | references/step-doc-sources.md | Yes |
| 6 | Validate | references/validate.md | Conditional |
| 7 | Generate Artifacts | references/generate-artifacts.md | Yes |
| 8 | Report | references/report.md | Yes |
| 9 | Workflow Health Check | references/health-check.md | Yes |

*Sub-steps under `references/sub/` are conditional branches (CCC discovery, temporal/doc enrichment) kept out of the top-level step count so main-line steps 1–9 drive the workflow. Step 3d (Component Extraction) stays top-level as an alternative main step that replaces the standard extraction path when `scope.type: "component-library"`.*

## Invocation Contract

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| **Inputs** | brief_path (path to skill-brief.yaml) [required], --batch [optional] |
| **Gates** | step 2: Choice Gate [P] (if match) | step 3: Review Gate [C] |
| **Outputs** | SKILL.md, context-snippet.md, metadata.json, provenance-map.json, evidence-report.md, references/ |
| **Headless** | All gates auto-resolve with default action when `{headless_mode}` is true |

## On Activation

1. Load config from `{project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml` and resolve:
   - `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`, `sidecar_path`, `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`

2. **Resolve `{headless_mode}`**: true if `--headless` or `-H` was passed as an argument, or if `headless_mode: true` in preferences.yaml. Default: false.

3. Load, read the full file, and then execute `references/load-brief.md` to begin the workflow.

88	- `content_hash` ← `content_hash` (direct copy, already in `sha256:{hexdigest}` format from story 1.2)

103	```

110	- Partial: `"Doc sources: detection partial — {N} found, {errors}"`

<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->

# Step 5a: Doc Sources

## STEP GOAL:

Record detected documentation pages and README with content hashes in metadata.json so that downstream audit (analyze-skill) can detect when upstream docs have changed since the skill was compiled.

## Rules

- Auto-proceed step — no user interaction required
- Graceful failure — if doc detection fails, skip with a warning and proceed to validate
- Do not modify any compiled artifact other than `metadata.json`
- Do not block the pipeline on any doc detection error

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 1. Check for Upstream Doc Detection Results

Check if `doc_detection_results` is already populated in the workflow context (set by BS auto-brief in the deepwiki pipeline).

- **If upstream results exist:** use them directly, skip to step 3.
- **If no upstream results:** continue to step 2.

### 2. Run Doc Detection (if needed)

Check that `{source_repo}` is available from the skill brief.

**If `{source_repo}` is not available:**
- Set `doc_detection_results` to an empty array `[]`
- Add evidence note: `"Doc sources: detection skipped — no source_repo in brief"`
- Skip to step 3.

**If `{source_repo}` is available:**

Invoke the detect-docs script:

```bash
uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-detect-docs.py \
  --repo-url {source_repo} \
  [--local-path {source_path}] \
  [--skip-pages-api]
```

Pass `--local-path {source_path}` when a local clone exists from the extraction step to avoid redundant cloning for `docs/` folder detection.

**Handle exit codes:**
- **Exit 0** (found ≥1 doc): parse JSON stdout as `doc_detection_results`
- **Exit 1** (none found): set `doc_detection_results` to empty array `[]`
- **Exit 2** (error): set `doc_detection_results` to empty array `[]`, add evidence note: `"Doc sources: detection partial — skf-detect-docs.py error (exit 2)"`

### 3. Ensure README Entry

After obtaining detection results, check if any entry has a URL matching `*/README.md` or `*/readme.md`.

**If a README entry already exists** from detection (e.g., `detected_via: "docs_folder"` found a README): keep it as-is.

**If no README entry exists,** add one:

- `url`: Construct from `{source_repo}/blob/main/README.md`
- `detected_via`: `"readme_always"`
- `content_hash`: Hash the README content using `sha256:{hexdigest}` convention:
  - If `{source_path}` is available: read `{source_path}/README.md` locally with `encoding="utf-8"`, compute `sha256:{hexdigest}`
  - Else: fetch via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/readme --jq '.content'`, base64-decode, hash
  - If README cannot be found or fetched: set `content_hash` to `null`
- `recorded_at`: current ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone

### 4. Build doc_sources Array

Map each detection result to the `doc_sources` schema:

```json
{
  "url": "{url from detection result}",
  "detected_via": "{detected_via from detection result}",
  "content_hash": "{content_hash from detection result — sha256:{hexdigest} or null}",
  "recorded_at": "{current ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone}"
}
```

Field mapping from `skf-detect-docs.py` output:
- `url` ← `url` (direct copy)
- `detected_via` ← `detected_via` (direct copy; or `"readme_always"` for the mandatory README entry)
- `content_hash` ← `content_hash` (direct copy, already in `sha256:{hexdigest}` format from story 1.2)
- `recorded_at` ← generated at step execution time (ISO-8601 with timezone)

Note: `content_type` from detect-docs output is NOT carried into `doc_sources`.

### 5. Update metadata.json

Read the staging `_bmad-output/{skill-name}/metadata.json` that compile (step 5) wrote.

**If the staging metadata.json is unreadable:** HALT — this indicates compile failed (critical, not doc-detection-related).

**Replace** the `doc_sources` field entirely (do not merge or append to stale data from prior compiles):

```python
metadata["doc_sources"] = new_doc_sources  # full replacement
```

Write the updated metadata.json back to the staging directory.

Add evidence note summarizing the result:
- Success: `"Doc sources: {N} detected, README tracked"`
- Skip: `"Doc sources: detection skipped — {reason}"`
- Partial: `"Doc sources: detection partial — {N} found, {errors}"`

### 6. Auto-Proceed

Load, read the entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.

5	# Resolve `{atomicWriteHelper}` by probing `{atomicWriteProbeOrder}` in order
6	# (installed SKF module path first, src/ dev-checkout fallback); first existing
7	# path wins. HALT if neither resolves — losing atomic-write guarantees is not
8	# an option for the staging-directory artifacts this step produces.
9	atomicWriteProbeOrder:
10	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-atomic-write.py'
11	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-atomic-write.py'
12	# Resolve `{descriptionGuardHelper}` by probing `{descriptionGuardProbeOrder}`
13	# in order (installed SKF module path first, src/ dev-checkout fallback);
14	# first existing path wins. HALT if neither resolves — letting an external
15	# tool's rewrite of the description field stand would silently regress
16	# discovery quality.
17	descriptionGuardProbeOrder:
18	  - '{project-root}/_bmad/skf/shared/scripts/skf-description-guard.py'
19	  - '{project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-description-guard.py'
20	---
21	
22	<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->
23	
24	# Step 6: Validate
25	
26	## STEP GOAL:
27	
28	To validate the compiled SKILL.md content against the agentskills.io specification using skill-check, auto-fix any validation failures, and confirm spec compliance before artifact generation.
29	
30	## Rules
31	
32	- Focus only on validating compiled content against spec — only fix spec compliance issues
33	- Validation and auto-fix modify files in the staging directory
34	- `<staging-skill-dir>` resolves to `_bmad-output/{skill-name}/` as created by step 5. The directory name must match the skill's frontmatter `name` field exactly — `skill-check`'s `frontmatter.name_matches_directory` rule rejects any suffix.
35	- If skill-check unavailable: skip validation, add warning to evidence report
36	- Ignore non-zero exit codes from skill-check if JSON output shows 0 errors
37	
38	## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
39	
40	### 0. Description Guard Protocol
41	
42	**Used by:** §2 (`skill-check check --fix`), §4 (`split-body`), and any future tool invocation that may modify SKILL.md.
43	
44	Load `{descriptionGuardProtocol}` for the full prose explanation of the four-phase guard (why it exists, what counts as divergence, why token-stream comparison is the right shape). The deterministic phases are executed via `{descriptionGuardHelper}` — the calling sections (§2 and §4) invoke the helper at the capture and verify-restore points.
45	
46	**This skill's post-restore re-validation hook:** after `{descriptionGuardHelper}` reports `restored: true`, run `uv run {project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-frontmatter.py <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md` and capture `schema_revalidation_result` in context. If the validator exits non-zero OR reports failure for the `description` field, flip the Schema result back to `FAIL` in the evidence report (overriding any prior PASS/WARN from §2), record `description_guard_revalidation: FAIL` with the validator's diagnostic message, and continue — do not halt (step 9 health-check and result contract still need to run so the failure is surfaced through the normal artifact path).
47	
48	### 1. Check Tool Availability
49	
50	Run: `timeout 30s npx skill-check -h` — the short timeout protects against a cold `npx` download blocking the workflow indefinitely on a slow network.
51	
52	- If succeeds: Continue to automated validation (section 2)
53	- If fails or times out: Perform manual fallback (section 3); add note to evidence-report: "Spec validation performed manually — skill-check tool unavailable". Also set `metadata.validation_status: 'manual-only'` in `metadata.json` (write via `python3 {atomicWriteHelper} write --target <staging-skill-dir>/metadata.json`), and in the evidence-report's `Validation Results` section mark Security, Body, and Content Quality (tessl) rows explicitly as `skipped — skill-check unavailable`. Downstream consumers (pipeline, forger, test-skill) check `validation_status` to decide how much weight to put on the artifact; leaving it unset would make a manual-only run look equivalent to a fully automated PASS.
54	
55	**Important:** Do not assume availability — empirical check required.
56	
57	### 2. Validate & Auto-Fix (skill-check check --fix)
58	
59	Run the external skill-check tool against the compiled skill staging directory.
60	
61	**Flag probe (run once, cache the result for §4 and §5 re-invocations):**
62	
63	```bash
64	npx skill-check check --help 2>/dev/null | grep -- --no-security-scan
65	```
66	
67	- If the probe matches `--no-security-scan`: set `{security_scan_flag} = "--no-security-scan"`.
68	- Else run a second probe — `npx skill-check check --help 2>/dev/null | grep -- --skip-security` — and if it matches, set `{security_scan_flag} = "--skip-security"`.
69	- If neither flag exists: set `{security_scan_flag} = ""` (empty) AND set `{skill_check_flag_fallback} = true`. Skip §2 and §4 automated flows entirely — fall through to §3 manual frontmatter validation. Record in evidence-report: `skill_check_flag_probe: neither --no-security-scan nor --skip-security supported by installed skill-check; validation performed manually`.
70	
71	**If a security-scan-disable flag was resolved (probe succeeded):**
72	
73	```bash
74	npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --fix --format json {security_scan_flag}
75	```
76	
77	This performs frontmatter validation, description quality checks, body limit enforcement, local link resolution, file formatting, auto-fix of deterministic issues, and quality scoring (0-100) across five weighted categories.
78	
79	**Parse the JSON output** for: `scores[].score` (0-100 — match the entry by `relativePath`/`skillId`; falls back to a top-level `qualityScore` on older skill-check builds), `diagnostics[]` (remaining issues), `fixed[]` (auto-corrected issues).
80	
81	**Description Guard Protocol:** This invocation may modify SKILL.md (especially when `fixed[]` is non-empty). Wrap the `skill-check check --fix` call in the four-phase guard defined in §0 by invoking `{descriptionGuardHelper}` at the capture and verify-restore points:
82	
83	```bash
84	# Phase 1 — capture before the tool call
85	uv run {descriptionGuardHelper} capture <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md
86	# stash the returned `description` as `guarded_description` in workflow context
87	
88	# Phase 2 — run skill-check (see command block above)
89	
90	# Phases 3+4 — verify and restore after the tool call
91	uv run {descriptionGuardHelper} verify-restore <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md \
92	    --captured-description "{guarded_description}"
93	```
94	
95	If `restored: true` in the verify-restore output, apply §0's post-restore re-validation hook. If `fixed[]` was non-empty in the skill-check output, also re-read the modified SKILL.md to sync the in-context copy before proceeding — this prevents silent divergence between the in-context and on-disk versions that step 7 will use for artifact generation.
96	
97	**Note:** `skill-check` may return non-zero exit code even when `summary.errorCount` is 0. Always rely on parsed JSON, not the shell exit code.
98	
99	- **Score ≥ 70:** Record "Schema: PASS (score: {score}/100)" in evidence-report
100	- **Score < 70:** Log remaining diagnostics as warnings, record "Schema: WARN — score {score}/100, {count} remaining issues", proceed
101	- **Unfixable errors:** Record specific rule IDs and suggestions, proceed with warnings
102	
103	### 3. Validate Frontmatter (Fallback)
104	
105	**If skill-check was available:** Skip — already validated in step 2.
106	
107	**If skill-check NOT available (fallback):** Perform manual frontmatter compliance check:
108	
109	- [ ] Frontmatter present — file starts with `---` and has closing `---`
110	- [ ] `name` field — present, non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, 1-64 chars
111	- [ ] `name` matches skill output directory name
112	- [ ] `description` field — present, non-empty, 1-1024 characters
113	- [ ] No unknown fields — only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` permitted
114	- [ ] `version` and `author` are NOT in frontmatter (they belong in metadata.json)
115	
116	If fails: auto-fix (deterministic), re-validate once, record result. If passes: record "Frontmatter: PASS".
117	
118	### 4. Split Oversized Body (if needed)
119	
120	**If step 2 reported `body.max_lines` failure:**
121	
122	**Description Guard Protocol:** Split operations may rewrite the frontmatter. Wrap the split invocation in the four-phase guard defined in §0:
123	
124	```bash
125	# Phase 1 — capture before the split
126	uv run {descriptionGuardHelper} capture <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md
127	# stash returned `description` as `guarded_description`
128	
129	# Phase 2 — run the split (selective extraction or, last-resort, split-body --write)
130	
131	# Phases 3+4 — verify and restore after the split
132	uv run {descriptionGuardHelper} verify-restore <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md \
133	    --captured-description "{guarded_description}"
134	```
135	
136	If `restored: true` in the verify-restore output, apply §0's post-restore re-validation hook.
137	
138	**Mandatory approach — selective split:** Identify Tier 2 sections by their `## Full` heading prefix (e.g., `## Full API Reference`, `## Full Type Definitions`, `## Full Integration Patterns`). Extract ONLY those sections to `references/`, starting with the largest. Keep ALL Tier 1 content and any smaller sections inline. Inline passive context achieves 100% task accuracy vs 79% for on-demand retrieval (per Vercel research).
139	
140	**FORBIDDEN:** Running `npx skill-check split-body --write` without prior selective extraction. The `split-body --write` command extracts ALL `##` sections top-to-bottom, destroying Tier 1 inline content that the two-tier design depends on. This command is a LAST RESORT only after selective split has been attempted and proven insufficient.
141	
142	**If selective split alone does not bring body under the limit** (rare — typically only occurs when Tier 1 itself exceeds 300 lines): reduce Tier 1 Key API Summary and Architecture at a Glance sections to fit within limits. Do NOT fall back to automated `split-body --write` to solve a Tier 1 sizing problem.
143	
144	**Tier 1 preservation check:** After ANY split operation, verify that ALL of the following sections remain inline in SKILL.md (not moved to references/): Overview, Quick Start, Common Workflows, Key API Summary, Migration & Deprecation Warnings (if present), Key Types, Architecture at a Glance, CLI (if present), Scripts & Assets (if present), Manual Sections. If any Tier 1 section was moved to references/, restore it immediately and re-split targeting only Tier 2 sections.
145	
146	**Post-split Tier-1 count check (mandatory):** before invoking the splitter, count the Tier-1 `##`-level section headings present in SKILL.md (any heading whose title matches one of the Tier-1 names listed above) and store as `tier1_count_pre`. After the split completes, recount as `tier1_count_post`. **HALT** if `tier1_count_post < tier1_count_pre` with: "Split reduced Tier-1 section count from {pre} to {post}. Tier-1 sections must remain inline. Restoring from staging backup and aborting body split — manual review required." Do not proceed past §4 — Tier-1 preservation is a hard invariant and a count drop indicates the splitter pulled an inline section into references/ regardless of the section-list check above (e.g., heading-text variation, capitalization, or the splitter's own heuristics).
147	
148	**Anchor validation and remediation:** After any split, verify that context-snippet section anchors (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`) still resolve to headings in SKILL.md. If an anchor no longer resolves (section was split out), restore that section to SKILL.md inline content — the context-snippet must always reference sections that exist in the main file.
149	
150	Then re-validate: `npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json {security_scan_flag}` — use the flag cached from §2's probe. If `{skill_check_flag_fallback}` is true, skip re-validation and rely on the §3 manual check.
151	
152	**If skill-check unavailable or no body size issue:** Skip.
153	
154	### 5. Security Scan
155	
156	**If skill-check available:**
157	
158	```bash
159	npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json
160	```
161	
162	(Security scan enabled by default when `--no-security-scan` omitted. The scan uses [Snyk](https://docs.snyk.io/) to check for prompt injection risks, sensitive data exposure, and unsafe tool permissions.)
163	
164	Record any security warnings in evidence-report. Security findings are advisory — they do not block artifact generation. If the full validation re-run produces a different quality score than section 2, update the evidence-report with the newer score.
165	
166	**If security scan fails due to missing SNYK_TOKEN:**
167	
168	Display: "Security scan requires a Snyk Enterprise API token ([docs](https://docs.snyk.io/snyk-api/authentication-for-api)). Set `SNYK_TOKEN=your-token` in environment or `.env`, then re-run [SF] Setup Forge. Without Enterprise, use `--no-security-scan` to skip. Security scanning is optional and does not block skill compilation."
169	
170	Record: "Security scan skipped — SNYK_TOKEN not configured"
171	
172	**If skill-check unavailable:** Skip with note: "Security scan skipped — skill-check tool unavailable"
173	
174	### 6. Content Quality Review (tessl)
175	
176	**If tessl available**, run: `timeout 120s npx -y tessl skill review <staging-skill-dir>` — the 120s cap matches skf-test-skill's tessl invocation guard and prevents a stalled LLM call in tessl from blocking compilation. On timeout, treat the step as unavailable and record `tessl: timeout — content quality review skipped` in the evidence report.
177	
178	Parse output for: `description_score`, `content_score`, `review_score`, `validation_result`, `judge_suggestions[]`.
179	
180	**Load dismissal rules:** Before interpreting any findings, load `{tesslDismissalData}` completely. This file is the single source of truth for tessl findings that SKF expects and must dismiss. It defines score thresholds, suggestion dismissal patterns, and the action to take when each rule matches.
181	
182	**Apply dismissal rules** in this order:
183	
184	1. **Check score thresholds** against the "Score Thresholds" table in `{tesslDismissalData}`. Most importantly:
185	   - If tessl's output contains a `findings[]` entry with rule ID `description_field` (the deterministic angle-bracket / XML-tag validator): follow the **recover-then-halt** path defined by the `description-xml-tags-guarded-upstream` rule in `{tesslDismissalData}`. Re-apply step 5 §2a's `<`/`>` → `{`/`}` substitution in place on the staging SKILL.md frontmatter `description`, re-sync the in-context copy, and re-run `npx -y tessl skill review <staging-skill-dir>` once. **Re-run gate:** treat the `description_field` finding being **absent** from the re-run as the only successful recovery outcome. If the finding persists on the re-run — whether the re-substitution improved the description or not — that counts as recovery failure: halt with the original `description-xml-tags-guarded-upstream` failure message from `{tesslDismissalData}`, do NOT proceed to §6b, and do NOT downgrade the recovery to a warning. On successful recovery (finding cleared), log `description-recovery: applied ({count} substitutions)` in the evidence report under "Dismissed tessl suggestions" and continue suggestion iteration against the rerun's `judge_suggestions[]`.
186	   - If the LLM-judge `description_score` is below 100 **but no `description_field` finding is present** (the deterministic validator PASSED): this is a soft discoverability signal (jargon density, trigger-term phrasing) from the judge's sub-scores, not a sanitizer bypass. Record `description_judge_score: {n}% (deterministic description_field validator PASSED)` as a warning in the evidence report and continue — do NOT trigger the recover-then-halt path and do NOT halt.
187	   - If `review_score < 60` or `content_score < 60`: record warnings in the evidence report, continue.
188	2. **Iterate `judge_suggestions[]`.** For each suggestion:
189	   - Cross-reference against the rules in `{tesslDismissalData}` in order.
190	   - If a rule matches: record `{rule_id, rationale, suggestion_text}` under "Dismissed tessl suggestions" in the evidence report. Do not apply.
191	   - If no rule matches: add to the "Novel tessl suggestions" list for §6b to surface to the user.
192	3. **Short-circuit when empty.** If every suggestion was dismissed (no novel suggestions), §6b has nothing to show — auto-proceed to §7.
193	
194	- **Unavailable:** Skip with note: "Content quality review skipped — tessl tool unavailable"
195	
196	tessl installs automatically via `npx`. A missing tool is not an error — graceful skip.

198	#### 6b. User Decision Gate (conditional)
199	
200	**If §6 produced no novel suggestions (all dismissed via `{tesslDismissalData}`) OR tessl was unavailable:** Skip this gate — auto-proceed.
201	
202	**If §6 produced novel suggestions** (ones not matched by any dismissal rule), present them to the user:
203	
204	"**Content quality review: {score}%**
205	
206	tessl suggestions (novel — not matched by `{tesslDismissalData}`):
207	{numbered list of novel suggestions}
208	
209	**Select an option:**
210	- **[S] Skip** — proceed with current content as-is (default)
211	- **[A] Apply structural fixes** — apply only structural suggestions (split sections, consolidate duplicates). No new content generated.
212	- **[R] Review all** — show each suggestion with proposed changes before applying"
213	
214	#### Gate Rules:
215	
216	- **Structural suggestions** (split reference section, consolidate duplicates, reorder sections) can be applied without zero-hallucination risk — they restructure existing content
217	- **Semantic suggestions** (add examples, add error handling, add validation checkpoints) introduce content not verified from source code. If the user chooses to apply these:
218	  - Warn: "This adds content not verified from source code."
219	  - Mark applied content with `<!-- [TESSL:auto-fix] -->` markers
220	  - Cite as `[TESSL:suggestion]` in the provenance map with `confidence: "TESSL"` (below T3)
221	  - Record in evidence report: "TESSL-suggested content applied: {count} items (unverified)"
222	- **If user selects [S]:** Record "tessl suggestions: skipped by user" in evidence report. Proceed to section 7.
223	- **If user selects [A]:** Apply structural fixes only, re-run tessl to capture updated score, record results. Proceed to section 7.
224	- **If user selects [R]:** Show each suggestion with the proposed change. For each, user confirms or skips. Apply confirmed changes, record results. Proceed to section 7.
225	
226	### 7. Validate metadata.json
227	
228	Cross-check metadata.json against extraction inventory:
229	- `stats.exports_documented` / `stats.exports_public_api` / `stats.exports_internal` / `stats.exports_total` are accurate

231	- `confidence_distribution.t1`, `confidence_distribution.t1_low`, `confidence_distribution.t2`, `confidence_distribution.t3` match actual counts
232	- `spec_version` is "1.3"
233	- If `scripts[]` or `assets[]` arrays present: verify `stats.scripts_count`/`stats.assets_count` match array lengths; verify `file_entries` count in provenance-map.json matches
234	
235	Auto-fix any discrepancies (these are computed values).
236	
237	### 8. Update Evidence Report

244	- Frontmatter: {pass/fail}
245	- Body: {pass/fail} {split-body applied if applicable}

<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->

# Step 6: Validate

## STEP GOAL:

To validate the compiled SKILL.md content against the agentskills.io specification using skill-check, auto-fix any validation failures, and confirm spec compliance before artifact generation.

## Rules

- Focus only on validating compiled content against spec — only fix spec compliance issues
- Validation and auto-fix modify files in the staging directory
- `<staging-skill-dir>` resolves to `_bmad-output/{skill-name}/` as created by step 5. The directory name must match the skill's frontmatter `name` field exactly — `skill-check`'s `frontmatter.name_matches_directory` rule rejects any suffix.
- If skill-check unavailable: skip validation, add warning to evidence report
- Ignore non-zero exit codes from skill-check if JSON output shows 0 errors

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 0. Description Guard Protocol

**Used by:** §2 (`skill-check check --fix`), §4 (`split-body`), and any future tool invocation that may modify SKILL.md.

Load `{descriptionGuardProtocol}` for the full prose explanation of the four-phase guard (why it exists, what counts as divergence, why token-stream comparison is the right shape). The deterministic phases are executed via `{descriptionGuardHelper}` — the calling sections (§2 and §4) invoke the helper at the capture and verify-restore points.

**This skill's post-restore re-validation hook:** after `{descriptionGuardHelper}` reports `restored: true`, run `uv run {project-root}/src/shared/scripts/skf-validate-frontmatter.py <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md` and capture `schema_revalidation_result` in context. If the validator exits non-zero OR reports failure for the `description` field, flip the Schema result back to `FAIL` in the evidence report (overriding any prior PASS/WARN from §2), record `description_guard_revalidation: FAIL` with the validator's diagnostic message, and continue — do not halt (step 9 health-check and result contract still need to run so the failure is surfaced through the normal artifact path).

### 1. Check Tool Availability

Run: `timeout 30s npx skill-check -h` — the short timeout protects against a cold `npx` download blocking the workflow indefinitely on a slow network.

- If succeeds: Continue to automated validation (section 2)
- If fails or times out: Perform manual fallback (section 3); add note to evidence-report: "Spec validation performed manually — skill-check tool unavailable". Also set `metadata.validation_status: 'manual-only'` in `metadata.json` (write via `python3 {atomicWriteHelper} write --target <staging-skill-dir>/metadata.json`), and in the evidence-report's `Validation Results` section mark Security, Body, and Content Quality (tessl) rows explicitly as `skipped — skill-check unavailable`. Downstream consumers (pipeline, forger, test-skill) check `validation_status` to decide how much weight to put on the artifact; leaving it unset would make a manual-only run look equivalent to a fully automated PASS.

**Important:** Do not assume availability — empirical check required.

### 2. Validate & Auto-Fix (skill-check check --fix)

Run the external skill-check tool against the compiled skill staging directory.

**Flag probe (run once, cache the result for §4 and §5 re-invocations):**

```bash
npx skill-check check --help 2>/dev/null | grep -- --no-security-scan
```

- If the probe matches `--no-security-scan`: set `{security_scan_flag} = "--no-security-scan"`.
- Else run a second probe — `npx skill-check check --help 2>/dev/null | grep -- --skip-security` — and if it matches, set `{security_scan_flag} = "--skip-security"`.
- If neither flag exists: set `{security_scan_flag} = ""` (empty) AND set `{skill_check_flag_fallback} = true`. Skip §2 and §4 automated flows entirely — fall through to §3 manual frontmatter validation. Record in evidence-report: `skill_check_flag_probe: neither --no-security-scan nor --skip-security supported by installed skill-check; validation performed manually`.

**If a security-scan-disable flag was resolved (probe succeeded):**

```bash
npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --fix --format json {security_scan_flag}
```

This performs frontmatter validation, description quality checks, body limit enforcement, local link resolution, file formatting, auto-fix of deterministic issues, and quality scoring (0-100) across five weighted categories.

**Parse the JSON output** for: `scores[].score` (0-100 — match the entry by `relativePath`/`skillId`; falls back to a top-level `qualityScore` on older skill-check builds), `diagnostics[]` (remaining issues), `fixed[]` (auto-corrected issues).

**Description Guard Protocol:** This invocation may modify SKILL.md (especially when `fixed[]` is non-empty). Wrap the `skill-check check --fix` call in the four-phase guard defined in §0 by invoking `{descriptionGuardHelper}` at the capture and verify-restore points:

```bash
# Phase 1 — capture before the tool call
uv run {descriptionGuardHelper} capture <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md
# stash the returned `description` as `guarded_description` in workflow context

# Phase 2 — run skill-check (see command block above)

# Phases 3+4 — verify and restore after the tool call
uv run {descriptionGuardHelper} verify-restore <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md \
    --captured-description "{guarded_description}"
```

If `restored: true` in the verify-restore output, apply §0's post-restore re-validation hook. If `fixed[]` was non-empty in the skill-check output, also re-read the modified SKILL.md to sync the in-context copy before proceeding — this prevents silent divergence between the in-context and on-disk versions that step 7 will use for artifact generation.

**Note:** `skill-check` may return non-zero exit code even when `summary.errorCount` is 0. Always rely on parsed JSON, not the shell exit code.

- **Score ≥ 70:** Record "Schema: PASS (score: {score}/100)" in evidence-report
- **Score < 70:** Log remaining diagnostics as warnings, record "Schema: WARN — score {score}/100, {count} remaining issues", proceed
- **Unfixable errors:** Record specific rule IDs and suggestions, proceed with warnings

### 3. Validate Frontmatter (Fallback)

**If skill-check was available:** Skip — already validated in step 2.

**If skill-check NOT available (fallback):** Perform manual frontmatter compliance check:

- [ ] Frontmatter present — file starts with `---` and has closing `---`
- [ ] `name` field — present, non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, 1-64 chars
- [ ] `name` matches skill output directory name
- [ ] `description` field — present, non-empty, 1-1024 characters
- [ ] No unknown fields — only `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools` permitted
- [ ] `version` and `author` are NOT in frontmatter (they belong in metadata.json)

If fails: auto-fix (deterministic), re-validate once, record result. If passes: record "Frontmatter: PASS".

### 4. Split Oversized Body (if needed)

**If step 2 reported `body.max_lines` failure:**

**Description Guard Protocol:** Split operations may rewrite the frontmatter. Wrap the split invocation in the four-phase guard defined in §0:

```bash
# Phase 1 — capture before the split
uv run {descriptionGuardHelper} capture <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md
# stash returned `description` as `guarded_description`

# Phase 2 — run the split (selective extraction or, last-resort, split-body --write)

# Phases 3+4 — verify and restore after the split
uv run {descriptionGuardHelper} verify-restore <staging-skill-dir>/SKILL.md \
    --captured-description "{guarded_description}"
```

If `restored: true` in the verify-restore output, apply §0's post-restore re-validation hook.

**Mandatory approach — selective split:** Identify Tier 2 sections by their `## Full` heading prefix (e.g., `## Full API Reference`, `## Full Type Definitions`, `## Full Integration Patterns`). Extract ONLY those sections to `references/`, starting with the largest. Keep ALL Tier 1 content and any smaller sections inline. Inline passive context achieves 100% task accuracy vs 79% for on-demand retrieval (per Vercel research).

**FORBIDDEN:** Running `npx skill-check split-body --write` without prior selective extraction. The `split-body --write` command extracts ALL `##` sections top-to-bottom, destroying Tier 1 inline content that the two-tier design depends on. This command is a LAST RESORT only after selective split has been attempted and proven insufficient.

**If selective split alone does not bring body under the limit** (rare — typically only occurs when Tier 1 itself exceeds 300 lines): reduce Tier 1 Key API Summary and Architecture at a Glance sections to fit within limits. Do NOT fall back to automated `split-body --write` to solve a Tier 1 sizing problem.

**Tier 1 preservation check:** After ANY split operation, verify that ALL of the following sections remain inline in SKILL.md (not moved to references/): Overview, Quick Start, Common Workflows, Key API Summary, Migration & Deprecation Warnings (if present), Key Types, Architecture at a Glance, CLI (if present), Scripts & Assets (if present), Manual Sections. If any Tier 1 section was moved to references/, restore it immediately and re-split targeting only Tier 2 sections.

**Post-split Tier-1 count check (mandatory):** before invoking the splitter, count the Tier-1 `##`-level section headings present in SKILL.md (any heading whose title matches one of the Tier-1 names listed above) and store as `tier1_count_pre`. After the split completes, recount as `tier1_count_post`. **HALT** if `tier1_count_post < tier1_count_pre` with: "Split reduced Tier-1 section count from {pre} to {post}. Tier-1 sections must remain inline. Restoring from staging backup and aborting body split — manual review required." Do not proceed past §4 — Tier-1 preservation is a hard invariant and a count drop indicates the splitter pulled an inline section into references/ regardless of the section-list check above (e.g., heading-text variation, capitalization, or the splitter's own heuristics).

**Anchor validation and remediation:** After any split, verify that context-snippet section anchors (`#quick-start`, `#key-types`) still resolve to headings in SKILL.md. If an anchor no longer resolves (section was split out), restore that section to SKILL.md inline content — the context-snippet must always reference sections that exist in the main file.

Then re-validate: `npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json {security_scan_flag}` — use the flag cached from §2's probe. If `{skill_check_flag_fallback}` is true, skip re-validation and rely on the §3 manual check.

**If skill-check unavailable or no body size issue:** Skip.

### 5. Security Scan

**If skill-check available:**

```bash
npx skill-check check <staging-skill-dir> --format json
```

(Security scan enabled by default when `--no-security-scan` omitted. The scan uses [Snyk](https://docs.snyk.io/) to check for prompt injection risks, sensitive data exposure, and unsafe tool permissions.)

Record any security warnings in evidence-report. Security findings are advisory — they do not block artifact generation. If the full validation re-run produces a different quality score than section 2, update the evidence-report with the newer score.

**If security scan fails due to missing SNYK_TOKEN:**

Display: "Security scan requires a Snyk Enterprise API token ([docs](https://docs.snyk.io/snyk-api/authentication-for-api)). Set `SNYK_TOKEN=your-token` in environment or `.env`, then re-run [SF] Setup Forge. Without Enterprise, use `--no-security-scan` to skip. Security scanning is optional and does not block skill compilation."

Record: "Security scan skipped — SNYK_TOKEN not configured"

**If skill-check unavailable:** Skip with note: "Security scan skipped — skill-check tool unavailable"

### 6. Content Quality Review (tessl)

**If tessl available**, run: `timeout 120s npx -y tessl skill review <staging-skill-dir>` — the 120s cap matches skf-test-skill's tessl invocation guard and prevents a stalled LLM call in tessl from blocking compilation. On timeout, treat the step as unavailable and record `tessl: timeout — content quality review skipped` in the evidence report.

Parse output for: `description_score`, `content_score`, `review_score`, `validation_result`, `judge_suggestions[]`.

**Load dismissal rules:** Before interpreting any findings, load `{tesslDismissalData}` completely. This file is the single source of truth for tessl findings that SKF expects and must dismiss. It defines score thresholds, suggestion dismissal patterns, and the action to take when each rule matches.

**Apply dismissal rules** in this order:

1. **Check score thresholds** against the "Score Thresholds" table in `{tesslDismissalData}`. Most importantly:
   - If tessl's output contains a `findings[]` entry with rule ID `description_field` (the deterministic angle-bracket / XML-tag validator): follow the **recover-then-halt** path defined by the `description-xml-tags-guarded-upstream` rule in `{tesslDismissalData}`. Re-apply step 5 §2a's `<`/`>` → `{`/`}` substitution in place on the staging SKILL.md frontmatter `description`, re-sync the in-context copy, and re-run `npx -y tessl skill review <staging-skill-dir>` once. **Re-run gate:** treat the `description_field` finding being **absent** from the re-run as the only successful recovery outcome. If the finding persists on the re-run — whether the re-substitution improved the description or not — that counts as recovery failure: halt with the original `description-xml-tags-guarded-upstream` failure message from `{tesslDismissalData}`, do NOT proceed to §6b, and do NOT downgrade the recovery to a warning. On successful recovery (finding cleared), log `description-recovery: applied ({count} substitutions)` in the evidence report under "Dismissed tessl suggestions" and continue suggestion iteration against the rerun's `judge_suggestions[]`.
   - If the LLM-judge `description_score` is below 100 **but no `description_field` finding is present** (the deterministic validator PASSED): this is a soft discoverability signal (jargon density, trigger-term phrasing) from the judge's sub-scores, not a sanitizer bypass. Record `description_judge_score: {n}% (deterministic description_field validator PASSED)` as a warning in the evidence report and continue — do NOT trigger the recover-then-halt path and do NOT halt.
   - If `review_score < 60` or `content_score < 60`: record warnings in the evidence report, continue.
2. **Iterate `judge_suggestions[]`.** For each suggestion:
   - Cross-reference against the rules in `{tesslDismissalData}` in order.
   - If a rule matches: record `{rule_id, rationale, suggestion_text}` under "Dismissed tessl suggestions" in the evidence report. Do not apply.
   - If no rule matches: add to the "Novel tessl suggestions" list for §6b to surface to the user.
3. **Short-circuit when empty.** If every suggestion was dismissed (no novel suggestions), §6b has nothing to show — auto-proceed to §7.

- **Unavailable:** Skip with note: "Content quality review skipped — tessl tool unavailable"

tessl installs automatically via `npx`. A missing tool is not an error — graceful skip.

#### 6b. User Decision Gate (conditional)

**If §6 produced no novel suggestions (all dismissed via `{tesslDismissalData}`) OR tessl was unavailable:** Skip this gate — auto-proceed.

**If §6 produced novel suggestions** (ones not matched by any dismissal rule), present them to the user:

"**Content quality review: {score}%**

tessl suggestions (novel — not matched by `{tesslDismissalData}`):
{numbered list of novel suggestions}

**Select an option:**
- **[S] Skip** — proceed with current content as-is (default)
- **[A] Apply structural fixes** — apply only structural suggestions (split sections, consolidate duplicates). No new content generated.
- **[R] Review all** — show each suggestion with proposed changes before applying"

#### Gate Rules:

- **Structural suggestions** (split reference section, consolidate duplicates, reorder sections) can be applied without zero-hallucination risk — they restructure existing content
- **Semantic suggestions** (add examples, add error handling, add validation checkpoints) introduce content not verified from source code. If the user chooses to apply these:
  - Warn: "This adds content not verified from source code."
  - Mark applied content with `<!-- [TESSL:auto-fix] -->` markers
  - Cite as `[TESSL:suggestion]` in the provenance map with `confidence: "TESSL"` (below T3)
  - Record in evidence report: "TESSL-suggested content applied: {count} items (unverified)"
- **If user selects [S]:** Record "tessl suggestions: skipped by user" in evidence report. Proceed to section 7.
- **If user selects [A]:** Apply structural fixes only, re-run tessl to capture updated score, record results. Proceed to section 7.
- **If user selects [R]:** Show each suggestion with the proposed change. For each, user confirms or skips. Apply confirmed changes, record results. Proceed to section 7.

### 7. Validate metadata.json

Cross-check metadata.json against extraction inventory:
- `stats.exports_documented` / `stats.exports_public_api` / `stats.exports_internal` / `stats.exports_total` are accurate
- `stats.public_api_coverage` and `stats.total_coverage` are correctly computed (null when denominator is 0)
- `confidence_distribution.t1`, `confidence_distribution.t1_low`, `confidence_distribution.t2`, `confidence_distribution.t3` match actual counts
- `spec_version` is "1.3"
- If `scripts[]` or `assets[]` arrays present: verify `stats.scripts_count`/`stats.assets_count` match array lengths; verify `file_entries` count in provenance-map.json matches

Auto-fix any discrepancies (these are computed values).

### 8. Update Evidence Report

Add validation results to evidence-report content in context:

```markdown
## Validation Results
- Schema: {pass/fail} (quality score: {score}/100)
- Frontmatter: {pass/fail}
- Body: {pass/fail} {split-body applied if applicable}
- Security: {pass/warn/skipped}
- Content Quality (tessl): {pass/warn/skipped} (score: {score}%)
- Metadata: {pass/fail}

## Quality Score Breakdown
- Frontmatter (30%): {score} | Description (30%): {score} | Body (20%): {score} | Links (10%): {score} | File (10%): {score}

## Description Guard
- Restored: {true/false}
- Triggering tool: {tool_name or —}
- Original description preserved: {true/false}
- Notes: {one-sentence detail or —}

## Auto-Fixed Issues
- {list of issues automatically corrected by --fix}

## Remaining Warnings / Security Findings / Content Quality (tessl)
- {warnings, security results, tessl scores and suggestions — or "skipped"}
```

**Description Guard population:** if the §0 protocol fired during §2 (`skill-check --fix`) or §4 (`split-body`), fill the four Description Guard fields from context:

- `Restored: true` when `description_guard_restored == true`, otherwise `false`.
- `Triggering tool`: the tool name recorded by §0 (`skill-check --fix`, `skill-check split-body`, etc.), or `—` if the guard did not fire.
- `Original description preserved`: `true` if the restore succeeded (on-disk now matches the pre-tool snapshot), `false` if restoration itself failed (rare — treat as a halt condition in a future version).
- `Notes`: a one-sentence description of what the tool had changed. Typical values: `"replaced with generic summary"`, `"truncated at N chars"`, `"angle-bracket tokens re-introduced"`, `"field deleted entirely"`. If `Restored: false`, use `—`.

When `Restored: false`, the three follow-up fields are all `—` — this is the clean-run expected state.

### 9. Menu Handling Logic

**Conditional interaction step.** If tessl produced suggestions, section 6b halts for user input. Otherwise, auto-proceed.

After validation completes (including any user decisions from section 6b), immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.

- Tool unavailability is a skip, not a halt
- Validation failures are warnings — proceed to artifact generation
- tessl gate only triggers when suggestions exist — no gate for clean reviews or unavailable tools

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN validation is complete (or skipped) and evidence-report content is updated will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for artifact generation.
